advice:
Take Amon-Ra St. Brown (WR, DET) with your pick now. Here's why:

**He's the best VORP-available player at your most urgent need.** You have 2 WR slots and 1 FLEX to fill, zero WRs on your roster, and St. Brown leads all available WRs with a VORP of 89.84 and a projected 247.54 points. That's a massive gap over the next WR on the board — Justin Jefferson at 61.64 VORP and Nico Collins at 44.52.

**Survivability is the real pressure here.** You have 5 picks until your next turn, and three of the four gap teams have already taken a WR in this draft. The WR tier at the top is thin — St. Brown, Jefferson, and then a steep drop. Jefferson (ADP 15.80) and St. Brown (ADP 12.60) are both in danger of disappearing before you pick again. St. Brown is the higher-VORP player and the safer grab right now.

**Zero-RB makes WR the priority.** Your strategy calls for loading up at receiver early. You already have James Cook anchoring your RB core. Locking in a true WR1 here is exactly what Zero-RB is designed to do — let you take elite pass-catchers while others reach for RBs.

**The risk of waiting one round is real.** Jefferson (VORP 61.64, ADP 15.80) could be gone. You'd be sliding to Nico Collins (VORP 44.52) or A.J. Brown (VORP 43.35) — a drop of roughly 46 VORP points from St. Brown. That's a significant hit to your team's ceiling for the sake of one round of patience.

One note on TE: McBride (VORP 70.85) and Bowers (VORP 67.25) are both elite options still on the board, but TE is a one-slot need and both have ADPs in the low 20s — they're more likely to survive a round than St. Brown. Lock in the WR1 now, and revisit TE next pick.
